title: How the Iran War Ignited a Clash Between Trump and the Saudi Crown Prince
summary: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman pressed President Trump earlier to cripple Iran. But as Iran asserted its power, the prince urged a cease-fire, and is now pursuing his security priorities.

title: U.S. and Iran Set to Hold Indirect Talks After Trading Attacks: What to Know
summary: Negotiators are in Qatar for indirect talks and ship traffic has risen in the Strait of Hormuz, even as Tehran and Washington remain far apart on key issues. Here’s what to know.

title: Trump Suggestion of a Syrian Crackdown on Hezbollah Confounds Many in Mideast
summary: During peace talks, President Trump repeatedly floated the idea that Syria could help subdue Hezbollah in Lebanon. The proposal revived bitter memories.

title: SpudCell: Scientists Made a Cell With Most of the Hallmarks of Life. Here’s What to Know.
summary: From why it was created to whether it’s alive, here’s what to know about SpudCell, the latest advance in synthetic biology.

title: Trump Pulled In About $1.4 Billion From Crypto Ventures, Financial Disclosure Shows
summary: The release of a mandatory financial disclosure for 2025 shows that the Trump family’s holdings, particularly the president’s crypto businesses, were stunningly lucrative.

title: Trump’s Moneymaking Run: Unrivaled in Presidential History
summary: The president’s move to open new business ventures, rather than eliminate potential conflicts, defies a long-held tradition.

title: As Earthquake Death Toll Mounts, Venezuela Grapples With Recovering and Burying Bodies
summary: As the death toll mounts, thousands of bodies have overwhelmed Venezuela’s forensic system, turning a seaport into a temporary morgue.

title: America, Onscreen
summary: Ten writers on the films that get at the hopes, dreams, heartbreak and hilarity of the U.S.A. as it turns 250.

title: N.Y.P.D. Memo Outlines Taylor Swift Wedding Events at the Garden
summary: Law enforcement officials in the city are preparing to monitor the pop star’s two-day event at Madison Square Garden, and several streets in the area will be closed.

title: What Would Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Prenuptial Agreement Look Like?
summary: What would a prenuptial agreement between one of the world’s biggest pop stars and a three-time Super Bowl champion look like? Legal experts weigh in.

title: Can ‘Chalant’ Dating Help More People Find Love? Ask Taylor and Travis.
summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are the poster children for enthusiastic romance. Some regular daters are pining for a similar dynamic.

title: The Democratic Incumbents Most at Risk of Losing to Progressive Primary Challengers in 2026
summary: A wave of outsider fervor has already knocked out a number of incumbents in primary races. Several more face spirited opposition.

title: Control of the Senate Is Up for Grabs, Times/Siena Polls Find
summary: Republicans are defending seats in Alaska, Iowa, Maine, North Carolina, Ohio and Texas as they try to maintain their majority. Democrats are competitive in all six states — but not leading in enough to take the chamber.

title: Despite Some Losses for Trump, Supreme Court Delivers Enduring Conservative Wins
summary: The justices pushed back on some of President Trump’s signature moves, but they also expanded presidential power and supplied victories on long-sought conservative goals.

title: The Birthright Decision Was Surprisingly Close, Some Legal Scholars Say
summary: A bare majority of Supreme Court justices ruled that President Trump’s executive order was unconstitutional, reflecting a conservative shift on the issue.

title: Fact-Checking Trump’s Inaccurate Accounts of U.S. History
summary: We fact-checked the president’s colorful — and inaccurate — accounts of American history from the Battle of Gettysburg to the Unabomber.

title: Fed’s Warsh Says Inflation Poses Less Risk Than a Few Weeks Ago
summary: The Federal Reserve chairman, Kevin Warsh, stressed that the central bank would “deliver price stability,” a point he has emphasized repeatedly since taking over in May.

title: The Divorce Crusader
summary: Part activist, part influencer, Adina Sash will do anything to rescue Orthodox women she sees as trapped in their marriages.

title: Troop Casualties in Ukraine War Top 2 Million, Study Finds
summary: Russia has borne the heavier toll, with 1.4 million troops killed or wounded since it invaded in February 2022.

title: A Town in Quebec Is the First Governing Body to Adopt the Universal Declaration of the Rights of Trees
summary: Terrasse-Vaudreuil has adopted a resolution to protect trees, which its mayor called “indispensable allies,” as living beings.

title: Five Words That Shook the World
summary: It wasn’t Thomas Jefferson who made “All men are created equal” actually mean something.

title: Is Kidmaxxing the Ultimate Status Symbol for Ultimate Wealth?
summary: What drives these men to reproduce at an industrial scale?

title: Two People Climb Needle of Empire State Building
summary: After making the climb, the pair unfurled a large black flag and apparently posted to Instagram. Video appears to show one person proposing to the other.
